ops.content:
name: Build Android APK

on: 
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'

      - name: Setup Gradle & Cache
        uses: gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v3

      - name: Build APK
        run: gradle assembleDebug

      - name: Upload APK
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: app-debug
          path: app/build/outputs/apk/debug/*.apk

# Add project specific ProGuard rules here.
-keepattributes *Annotation*
-keepattributes Signature

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
    
    <application
        android:allowBackup="false"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:theme="@android:style/Theme.DeviceDefault.NoActionBar">
        
        <activity 
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
    </application>
</manifest>

messages_preview.content:
### ROLE: CONDUIT ADAPTIVE SELF-HEALING ENGINE (LEVEL 10)

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL
1. WHITESPACE/INDENTATION: Completely ignore it. A match exists if the tokens and logic flow are identical.
2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.
3. CONTEXTUAL ANCHORING: If the target block is small (e.g., 1-2 lines), you MUST look at the surrounding lines in the 'File' context to ensure you aren't matching a duplicate symbol in a different function.
4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES
- The context provided to you contains line numbers followed by a ' | ' separator (e.g., " 25 | code").
- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.
- COORDINATE INTEGRITY: All coordinate fields are REQUIRED by the schema. If you are not using a specific field (e.g., you are doing a Range Replace and don't need 'anchor_line'), you MUST set the unused fields to 'null'.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION
- [STRATEGY: RANGE] Use 'start_line' + 'end_line'. Best for 'replace_block' failures. Ensure the range covers the ENTIRE logical block you want to overwrite.
- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).
- 0: No logical match exists.

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").
- 'can_fix': If you are guessing with less than 60% confidence, set this to false.

### STRICT MODE
- DO NOT RESPOND WITH TEXT.
- ONLY CALL THE suggest_fix FUNCTION.
- SILENCE IS MANDATORY.

ops.text: Fix GitHub 422 error: Cleanly overwrite workflow file with correct syntax

ops.content:
name: Heavy AI Worker

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      record_id:
        description: 'The Supabase record ID'
        required: true
      image_paths:
        description: 'JSON array of storage paths'
        required: true
      request_id:
        description: 'The tracking request ID'
        required: true

jobs:
  process:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Deno
        uses: denoland/setup-deno@v1
        with:
          deno-version: v1.x

      - name: Run AI Logic
        env:
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
          RECORD_ID: ${{ github.event.inputs.record_id }}
          IMAGE_PATHS: ${{ github.event.inputs.image_paths }}
          REQUEST_ID: ${{ github.event.inputs.request_id }}
        run: |
          deno run -A supabase/functions/upload-image/worker_logic.ts

operations.content:
name: Heavy AI Worker

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      record_id:
        description: 'The Supabase record ID'
        required: true
      image_paths:
        description: 'JSON array of storage paths'
        required: true
      request_id:
        description: 'The tracking request ID'
        required: true

jobs:
  process:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Deno
        uses: denoland/setup-deno@v1
        with:
          deno-version: v1.x

      - name: Run AI Logic
        env:
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
          RECORD_ID: ${{ github.event.inputs.record_id }}
          IMAGE_PATHS: ${{ github.event.inputs.image_paths }}
          REQUEST_ID: ${{ github.event.inputs.request_id }}
        run: |
          deno run -A supabase/functions/upload-image/worker_logic.ts

### MISSION
You are the final line of defense for a code-patching engine. A developer's 'find_block' failed to match the file because of minor discrepancies (whitespace, line order, variable renaming, or partial code degradation). Your goal is to identify the EXACT coordinates where the patch should have applied.

### ALGORITHMIC FUZZY MATCHING PROTOCOL

2. QUOTE/SYNTAX AGNOSTICISM: Treat ' and " as identical. Treat trailing commas as optional.

4. LOGICAL DRIFT: If the user's 'find_block' has 4 variables but the file has those 4 variables PLUS 1 new one in the middle, this is a MATCH. Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' must encompass the entire group.

### THE LAW OF COORDINATES

- Your 'start_line' and 'end_line' MUST refer to these numbers.

### OUTPUT STRATEGY SELECTION

- [STRATEGY: ANCHOR] Use 'anchor_line'. Best for 'insert_after' or 'insert_before' failures where the anchor moved but is still a single line.
- [STRATEGY: STRING] Use 'new_anchor_text'. Only use this if you find a unique line that is 100% stable and wasn't provided by the user.

### SCORE RUBRIC (confidence_score)
- 100: Identical logic, only whitespace or quote differences.
- 90-95: Identical logic, but 1-2 variables were renamed or a trailing comma was added/removed.
- 75-85: The structure matches but the file has 1-2 extra lines of code mixed in (logical drift).
- 60: You found a possible location but it's risky (multiple similar spots).

### DATA INTEGRITY CONSTRAINTS
- 'confidence_score': MUST be a WHOLE INTEGER (e.g., 95). No floats (0.95 is ILLEGAL).
- 'explanation': Technical, concise, and definitive. Max 30 words. Identify the exact mismatch (e.g., "Variables reversed on lines 24-25").

File:
  1 | package com.universal.app
  2 | 
  3 | import android.accessibilityservice.AccessibilityService
  4 | import android.accessibilityservice.GestureDescription
  5 | import android.content.Context
  6 | import android.content.Intent
  7 | import android.graphics.Color
  8 | import android.graphics.Path
  9 | import android.graphics.PixelFormat
 10 | import android.graphics.Rect
 11 | import android.os.Handler
 12 | import android.os.Looper
 13 | import android.os.PowerManager
 14 | import android.os.VibrationEffect
 15 | import android.os.Vibrator
 16 | import android.provider.MediaStore
 17 | import android.view.KeyEvent
 18 | import android.view.View
 19 | import android.view.WindowManager
 20 | import android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityEvent
 21 | import android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityNodeInfo
 22 | import java.util.LinkedList
 23 | 
 24 | class KeyInterceptService : AccessibilityService(), android.hardware.SensorEventListener {
 25 |     private val handler = Handler(Looper.getMainLooper())
 26 |     private lateinit var sensorManager: android.hardware.SensorManager
 27 |     private var accelerometer: android.hardware.Sensor? = null
 28 |     private var gravitySensor: android.hardware.Sensor? = null
 29 |     
 30 |     private var lastLinearAcc = floatArrayOf(0f, 0f, 0f)
 31 |     private var lastGravity = floatArrayOf(0f, 0f, 0f)
 32 |     
 33 |     private var isStabilizing = false
 34 |     private var stabilityStartTime = 0L
 35 |     private var captureCount = 0
 36 |     private var targetCaptureCount = 1
 37 |     private var blockerOverlay: View? = null
 38 |     private var lastHeadsetClick = 0L
 39 |     private var gestureSequence = ""
 40 |     private var lastHeadsetDownTime = 0L
 41 |     private val headsetGestureRunnable = Runnable { 
 42 |         processHeadsetGesture(gestureSequence)
 43 |         gestureSequence = ""
 44 |     }
 45 |     private var serviceWakeLock: PowerManager.WakeLock? = null
 46 |     private var lastUpTime = 0L
 47 |     private var upCount = 0
 48 |     private var lastDownTime = 0L
 49 |     private var downCount = 0
 50 |     
 51 |     private val CLICK_GAP = 400L
 52 | 
 53 |     private var isVolUpPressed = false
 54 |     private var isVolDownPressed = false
 55 |     private var isWaitingForTapHold = false
 56 |     private var isWaitingForDownTapHold = false
 57 |     private var lastCameraPackage = ""
 58 |     private var isLensSwitchPending = false
 59 |     private var isEarphoneNavMode = false
 60 |     private var isLongPressTriggered = false
 61 |     private var successiveRemaining = 0
 62 |     
 63 |     private val volDownBatchRunnable = Runnable {
 64 |         isLongPressTriggered = true
 65 |         val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
 66 |         if (prefs.getBoolean("touch_blocker", false)) {
 67 |             prefs.edit().putBoolean("touch_blocker", false).apply()
 68 |             removeTouchBlocker()
 69 |             speak("Emergency unlock: Touch restored", true)
 70 |             hapticPulse(500)
 71 |         } else {
 72 |             // Fallback: If blocker wasn't on, we can still use this for batch upload
 73 |             speak("Initiating batch upload", true)
 74 |             val intent = Intent(this@KeyInterceptService, ImageMonitorService::class.java).apply { action = "COMMAND_FLUSH_BATCH" }
 75 |             if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.O) startForegroundService(intent) else startService(intent)
 76 |         }
 77 |     }
 78 | 
 79 |     private val volUpLongPressRunnable = Runnable {
 80 |         isEarphoneNavMode = !isEarphoneNavMode
 81 |         isLongPressTriggered = true
 82 |         val status = if (isEarphoneNavMode) "Earphone Navigation Activated" else "Earphone Navigation Deactivated"
 83 |         DebugLogger.log("MODE", status)
 84 |         speak(status, true)
 85 |         hapticPulse(500)
 86 |         
 87 |         if (isEarphoneNavMode) {
 88 |             val intent = Intent(this@KeyInterceptService, PlaybackService::class.java).apply {
 89 |                 action = "START_NAV"
 90 |             }
 91 |             startService(intent)
 92 |         }
 93 |     }
 94 | 
 95 |     override fun onKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
 96 |         val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
 97 |         // Absolute first check - if inactive, pass through immediately
 98 |         if (!prefs.getBoolean("is_active", true)) return false
 99 | 
100 |         val pm = getSystemService(Context.POWER_SERVICE) as PowerManager
101 |         val isScreenOn = pm.isInteractive
102 |         if (!prefs.getBoolean("keys_enabled", true)) return false
103 | 
104 |         val keyCode = event.keyCode
105 |         val action = event.action
106 |         val isLongPress = (event.eventTime - event.downTime) > 600
107 | 
108 |         // 1. Camera Context & Emergency Logic
109 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow
110 |         val pkg = root?.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
111 |         val isCamOpen = pkg.contains("camera") || pkg.contains("lens")
112 | 
113 |         if (isCamOpen && (keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP || keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN)) {
114 |             // --- Emergency Blocker Removal (Priority 1) ---
115 |             if (keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP && action == KeyEvent.ACTION_UP) {
116 |                 val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
117 |                 if (now - lastUpTime < 400 && blockerOverlay != null) {
118 |                     DebugLogger.log("BLOCKER", "Emergency Removal via VolUp Double-Tap")
119 |                     removeTouchBlocker()
120 |                     return true // Consume to prevent shutter on emergency exit
121 |                 }
122 |                 lastUpTime = now
123 |                 // Fall through to check for shutter action
124 |             }
125 | 
126 |             // --- Shutter Trigger (Priority 2) ---
127 |             if (action == KeyEvent.ACTION_UP) {
128 |                 DebugLogger.log("SHUTTER", "Camera context: Intercepting volume key for capture.")
129 |                 smartShutterClick()
130 |                 return true // Consume the event so the native camera doesn't also fire.
131 |             }
132 | 
133 |             // For ACTION_DOWN or other events, pass them through to allow native camera functions like focus.
134 |             return false
135 |         }
136 | 
137 |         // 2. Headset Hijack Logic (Highest Priority)
138 |         val isHeadsetKey = keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_HEADSETHOOK || 
139 |                           keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_MEDIA_PLAY_PAUSE || 
140 |                           keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_MEDIA_NEXT || 
141 |                           keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_MEDIA_PREVIOUS
142 | 
143 |         if (isHeadsetKey) {
144 |             if (action == KeyEvent.ACTION_DOWN) {
145 |                 if (event.repeatCount == 0) {
146 |                     lastHeadsetDownTime = event.eventTime
147 |                     val pm = getSystemService(Context.POWER_SERVICE) as PowerManager
148 |                     val wl = pm.newWakeLock(PowerManager.PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK, "UniversalApp::KeyHijack")
149 |                     wl.acquire(500)
150 |                 }
151 |                 return true 
152 |             }
153 |             if (action == KeyEvent.ACTION_UP) {
154 |                 val duration = event.eventTime - lastHeadsetDownTime
155 |                 val type = if (duration > 600) "L" else "S"
156 |                 DebugLogger.log("HEADSET_RAW", "Press: ${duration}ms -> Added: $type")
157 |                 handler.removeCallbacks(headsetGestureRunnable)
158 |                 
159 |                 gestureSequence += type
160 |                 
161 |                 handler.postDelayed(headsetGestureRunnable, 450)
162 |                 return true
163 |             }
164 |         }
165 | 
166 |         // 3. Volume Key DOWN Logic
167 |         if (action == KeyEvent.ACTION_DOWN) {
168 |             val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
169 |             if (keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP) {
170 |                 isVolUpPressed = true
171 |                 isWaitingForTapHold = (now - lastUpTime < 800)
172 |             }
173 |             if (keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN) {
174 |                 isVolDownPressed = true
175 |                 isWaitingForDownTapHold = (now - lastDownTime < 800)
176 |             }
177 | 
178 |             if (event.repeatCount > 0) return true
179 |             if (isVolUpPressed && isVolDownPressed) return true
180 | 
181 |             when (keyCode) {
182 |                 KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP -> {
183 |                     if (event.repeatCount == 0) {
184 |                         isLongPressTriggered = false
185 |                         handler.postDelayed(volUpLongPressRunnable, 5000)
186 |                     }
187 |                     val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
188 |                     if (now - lastUpTime < CLICK_GAP) upCount++ else upCount = 1
189 |                     lastUpTime = now
190 |                     return true
191 |                 }
192 |                 KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN -> {
193 |                     if (event.repeatCount == 0) {
194 |                         isLongPressTriggered = false
195 |                         handler.postDelayed(volDownBatchRunnable, 5000)
196 |                     }
197 |                     val now = System.currentTimeMillis()
198 |                     if (now - lastDownTime < CLICK_GAP) downCount++ else downCount = 1
199 |                     lastDownTime = now
200 |                     return true
201 |                 }
202 |             }
203 |         }
204 | 
205 |         // 4. Volume Key UP Logic
206 |         if (action == KeyEvent.ACTION_UP) {
207 |             val wasBothPressed = isVolUpPressed && isVolDownPressed
208 |             if (keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP) isVolUpPressed = false
209 |             if (keyCode == KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN) isVolDownPressed = false
210 | 
211 |             if (wasBothPressed) {
212 |                 val intent = Intent(this, PlaybackService::class.java)
213 |                 intent.action = if (isLongPress) "RESET" else "PAUSE"
214 |                 startService(intent)
215 |                 return true
216 |             }
217 | 
218 | 
219 | 
220 |             when (keyCode) {
221 |                 KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_UP -> {
222 |                     handler.removeCallbacks(volUpLongPressRunnable)
223 |                     if (isLongPressTriggered) {
224 |                         isLongPressTriggered = false
225 |                         upCount = 0
226 |                         isWaitingForTapHold = false
227 |                         return true
228 |                     }
229 |                     
230 |                     val duration = event.eventTime - event.downTime
231 |                     if (isWaitingForTapHold && duration > 500) {
232 |                         toggleCamera()
233 |                         isWaitingForTapHold = false
234 |                         upCount = 0 
235 |                         return true 
236 |                     }
237 |                     isWaitingForTapHold = false
238 |                     handlePress("UP", upCount, isLongPress)
239 |                 }
240 |                 KeyEvent.KEYCODE_VOLUME_DOWN -> {
241 |                     handler.removeCallbacks(volDownBatchRunnable)
242 |                     if (isLongPressTriggered) return true
243 |                     
244 |                     val duration = event.eventTime - event.downTime
245 |                     if (isWaitingForDownTapHold && duration > 500) {
246 |                         triggerReset()
247 |                         isWaitingForDownTapHold = false
248 |                         downCount = 0
249 |                         return true
250 |                     }
251 |                     isWaitingForDownTapHold = false
252 |                     handlePress("DOWN", downCount, isLongPress)
253 |                 }
254 |             }
255 |             return true
256 |         }
257 | 
258 |         return super.onKeyEvent(event)
259 |     }
260 | 
261 |     private fun toggleCamera() {
262 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow
263 |         val currentPackage = root?.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
264 |         
265 |         if (currentPackage.contains("camera") || currentPackage.contains("lens") || currentPackage.contains("capture")) {
266 |             DebugLogger.log("CAM_TOGGLE", "Camera detected active. Closing.")
267 |             speak("Closing camera", true)
268 |             removeTouchBlocker()
269 |             performGlobalAction(GLOBAL_ACTION_HOME)
270 |         } else {
271 |             DebugLogger.log("CAM_TOGGLE", "Launching Hardware Camera. Starting Instant-Scan.")
272 |             speak("Opening camera", true)
273 |             
274 |             isLensSwitchPending = true
275 |             lastCameraPackage = "" 
276 |             
277 |             val intent = Intent(MediaStore.INTENT_ACTION_STILL_IMAGE_CAMERA)
278 |             intent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
279 |             startActivity(intent)
280 | 
281 |             // Start the search timer IMMEDIATELY, don't wait for accessibility events
282 |             handler.postDelayed({ 
283 |                 DebugLogger.log("LENS_FLOW", "Instant-Scan Timer Started")
284 |                 attemptLensSwitch(40) 
285 |             }, 1000)
286 |         }
287 |     }

289 |     private fun showTouchBlocker() {
290 |         if (blockerOverlay != null) return
291 |         val wm = getSystemService(Context.WINDOW_SERVICE) as WindowManager
292 |         blockerOverlay = View(this).apply {
293 |             setBackgroundColor(Color.TRANSPARENT)
294 |             setOnTouchListener { _, _ -> true }
295 |         }
296 | 
297 |         val params = WindowManager.LayoutParams(
298 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
299 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
300 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.TYPE_ACCESSIBILITY_OVERLAY,
301 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_NOT_FOCUSABLE or 
302 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_LAYOUT_IN_SCREEN or
303 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_LAYOUT_INSET_DECOR or
304 |             WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_WATCH_OUTSIDE_TOUCH,
305 |             PixelFormat.TRANSLUCENT
306 |         ).apply {
307 |             gravity = android.view.Gravity.TOP
308 |             // Full screen coverage including status bar and navigation area
309 |             height = WindowManager.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
310 |         }
311 |         
312 |         try {
313 |             wm.addView(blockerOverlay, params)
314 |             DebugLogger.log("BLOCKER", "Global Touch Blocker Active")
315 |             speak("System locked")
316 |         } catch (e: Exception) { DebugLogger.log("BLOCKER", "Error: ${e.message}") }
317 |     }
318 | 
319 |     private fun removeTouchBlocker() {
320 |         blockerOverlay?.let {
321 |             val wm = getSystemService(Context.WINDOW_SERVICE) as WindowManager
322 |             try { 
323 |                 wm.removeView(it) 
324 |                 speak("Touch input restored")
325 |             } catch (e: Exception) {}
326 |             blockerOverlay = null
327 |             DebugLogger.log("BLOCKER", "Touch Blocker Removed")
328 |         }
329 |     }
330 | 
331 |     private fun handlePress(key: String, count: Int, isLong: Boolean) {
332 |         DebugLogger.log("KEY_LOGIC", "Decided: $key (Count: $count, Long: $isLong)")
333 |         val intent = Intent(this, PlaybackService::class.java)
334 |         
335 |         if (key == "UP") {
336 |             when {
337 |                 isLong -> intent.apply { action = "PLAY_TYPE"; putExtra("type", "wo") }
338 |                 count == 1 -> intent.apply { action = "NEXT" }
339 |                 count == 2 -> intent.apply { action = "PLAY_TYPE"; putExtra("type", "tf") }
340 |                 count == 3 -> intent.apply { action = "PLAY_TYPE"; putExtra("type", "sa") }
341 |                 else -> { /* No action */ }
342 |             }
343 |         } else {
344 |             when {
345 |                 isLong -> intent.apply { action = "PLAY_TYPE"; putExtra("type", "fill") }
346 |                 count == 1 -> intent.apply { action = "PREVIOUS" }
347 |                 count == 2 -> intent.apply { action = "PLAY_TYPE"; putExtra("type", "mc") }
348 |                 count == 3 -> intent.apply { action = "PLAY_TYPE"; putExtra("type", "ma") }
349 |                 else -> { /* No action */ }
350 |             }
351 |         }
352 |         
353 |         if (intent.action != null) {
354 |             startService(intent)
355 |         }
356 |     }
357 | 
358 |     private fun prepareWideLens(): Boolean {
359 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return false
360 |         
361 |         var lensNode: AccessibilityNodeInfo? = null
362 |         val searchTerms = listOf(".5", "0.5", "0,5", "0.6", "0.5x", "0,5x", "ultra", "wide", "zoom out")
363 |         val bounds = Rect()
364 | 
365 |         val queue = LinkedList<AccessibilityNodeInfo>()
366 |         queue.add(root)
367 |         while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
368 |             val node = queue.poll() ?: continue
369 |             val text = node.text?.toString()?.lowercase() ?: ""
370 |             val desc = node.contentDescription?.toString()?.lowercase() ?: ""
371 |             
372 |             val match = searchTerms.find { (text.contains(it) || desc.contains(it)) && !text.contains("1x") }
373 | 
374 |             if (match != null) {
375 |                 var current: AccessibilityNodeInfo? = node
376 |                 var depth = 0
377 |                 while (current != null && depth < 6) {
378 |                     if (current.isClickable) {
379 |                         lensNode = current
380 |                         break
381 |                     }
382 |                     current = current.parent
383 |                     depth++
384 |                 }
385 |                 if (lensNode != null) break
386 |             }
387 |             for (i in 0 until node.childCount) { node.getChild(i)?.let { queue.add(it) } }
388 |         }
389 | 
390 |         return if (lensNode != null) {
391 |             // Try Virtual Click first (Robust Verification)
392 |             val success = lensNode.performAction(AccessibilityNodeInfo.ACTION_CLICK)
393 |             
394 |             if (success) {
395 |                 DebugLogger.log("LENS_FLOW", "Virtual click SUCCESS")
396 |                 speak("Wide lens activated", true)
397 |                 hapticPulse(50)
398 |                 true
399 |             } else {
400 |                 // Fallback to Physical Tap for Samsung/Google Camera
401 |                 lensNode.getBoundsInScreen(bounds)
402 |                 val x = bounds.centerX().toFloat()
403 |                 val y = bounds.centerY().toFloat()
404 |                 
405 |                 val clickPath = Path().apply { moveTo(x, y) }
406 |                 val gesture = GestureDescription.Builder()
407 |                     .addStroke(GestureDescription.StrokeDescription(clickPath, 0, 50))
408 |                     .build()
409 |                 
410 |                 dispatchGesture(gesture, null, null)
411 |                 DebugLogger.log("LENS_FLOW", "Virtual click REJECTED. Physical tap dispatched to $x, $y")
412 |                 // Return false so the retry loop continues to verify the switch
413 |                 false
414 |             }
415 |         } else {
416 |             false
417 |         }
418 |     }
419 | 
420 |     private fun attemptLensSwitch(retries: Int) {
421 |         if (!isLensSwitchPending) return
422 |         
423 |         if (retries <= 0) {
424 |             DebugLogger.log("LENS_FLOW", "Search timed out. Applying fallback coordinates.")
425 |             clickLensCoordinatesFallback()
426 |             isLensSwitchPending = false
427 |             val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
428 |             if (prefs.getBoolean("touch_blocker", false)) showTouchBlocker()
429 |             return
430 |         }
431 | 
432 |         handler.postDelayed({
433 |             if (!isLensSwitchPending) return@postDelayed
434 |             
435 |             val success = prepareWideLens()
436 |             if (success) {
437 |                 DebugLogger.log("LENS_FLOW", "Lens switch CONFIRMED.")
438 |                 isLensSwitchPending = false
439 |                 val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
440 |                 if (prefs.getBoolean("touch_blocker", false)) showTouchBlocker()
441 |             } else {
442 |                 attemptLensSwitch(retries - 1)
443 |             }
444 |         }, 500)
445 |     }
446 | 
447 |     private fun clickLensCoordinatesFallback() {
448 |         val metrics = resources.displayMetrics
449 |         // Standard ultra-wide button is usually centered but slightly above the shutter
450 |         val x = metrics.widthPixels / 2f
451 |         val y = metrics.heightPixels * 0.70f 
452 | 
453 |         val clickPath = Path().apply { moveTo(x, y) }
454 |         val gesture = GestureDescription.Builder()
455 |             .addStroke(GestureDescription.StrokeDescription(clickPath, 0, 50))
456 |             .build()
457 |         dispatchGesture(gesture, null, null)
458 |         hapticPulse(100)
459 |     }
460 | 
461 |     private fun performPinchFallback() {
462 |         val metrics = resources.displayMetrics
463 |         val centerX = metrics.widthPixels / 2f
464 |         val centerY = metrics.heightPixels / 2f
465 |         val path1 = android.graphics.Path().apply { moveTo(100f, 100f); lineTo(centerX - 50, centerY - 50) }
466 |         val path2 = android.graphics.Path().apply { moveTo(metrics.widthPixels - 100f, 100f); lineTo(centerX + 50, centerY - 50) }
467 |         val stroke1 = GestureDescription.StrokeDescription(path1, 0, 500)
468 |         val stroke2 = GestureDescription.StrokeDescription(path2, 0, 500)
469 |         val gesture = GestureDescription.Builder().addStroke(stroke1).addStroke(stroke2).build()
470 |         
471 |         dispatchGesture(gesture, object : AccessibilityService.GestureResultCallback() {
472 |             override fun onCompleted(gestureDescription: GestureDescription) = DebugLogger.log("GESTURE", "Pinch SUCCESS")
473 |             override fun onCancelled(gestureDescription: GestureDescription) = DebugLogger.log("GESTURE", "Pinch FAILED")
474 |         }, null)
475 |     }
476 | 
477 |     private fun startStabilityTrigger() {
478 |         val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
479 |         targetCaptureCount = if (prefs.getBoolean("successive_enabled", false)) prefs.getInt("successive_count", 3) else 1
480 |         captureCount = 0
481 |         isStabilizing = true
482 |         stabilityStartTime = 0
483 |         
484 |         sensorManager.registerListener(this, accelerometer, android.hardware.SensorManager.SENSOR_DELAY_GAME)
485 |         sensorManager.registerListener(this, gravitySensor, android.hardware.SensorManager.SENSOR_DELAY_GAME)
486 |         
487 |         DebugLogger.log("STABILIZER", "Started. Targets: $targetCaptureCount")
488 |         hapticPulse(30) // Initial engagement tick
489 |         startVibrationLoop()
490 |     }
491 | 
492 |     private fun startVibrationLoop() {
493 |         if (!isStabilizing) return
494 |         
495 |         // 1. Check Leveling
496 |         val tilt = Math.abs(lastGravity[2]) // Z-axis gravity
497 |         // If Z is < 8, the phone is tilted more than ~35 degrees
498 |         if (tilt < 7.5f) {
499 |             // Distinct 'Level Me' pattern
500 |             val v = getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
501 |             v.vibrate(VibrationEffect.createOneShot(100, 40))
502 |             handler.postDelayed({ startVibrationLoop() }, 1000)
503 |             return
504 |         }
505 | 
506 |         // 2. Calculate Jitter
507 |         val magnitude = Math.sqrt((lastLinearAcc[0] * lastLinearAcc[0] + lastLinearAcc[1] * lastLinearAcc[1] + lastLinearAcc[2] * lastLinearAcc[2]).toDouble()).toFloat()
508 |         
509 |         // 3. Map to Amplitude (Stealth Capped at 100/255)
510 |         // High movement (>1.0) = 100 amplitude. Low movement (<0.2) = 0 amplitude.
511 |         val amplitude = ((magnitude - 0.2f) * 120).toInt().coerceIn(0, 100)
512 |         
513 |         val v = getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
514 |         if (amplitude > 15) {
515 |             v.vibrate(VibrationEffect.createOneShot(100, amplitude))
516 |             stabilityStartTime = 0
517 |         } else {
518 |             // Hand is steady
519 |             if (stabilityStartTime == 0L) stabilityStartTime = System.currentTimeMillis()
520 |             
521 |             if (System.currentTimeMillis() - stabilityStartTime > 400) {
522 |                 // LOCKED: Capture Sequence
523 |                 executeStabilizedCapture()
524 |                 return
525 |             }
526 |         }
527 | 
528 |         handler.postDelayed({ startVibrationLoop() }, 120)
529 |     }
530 | 
531 |     private fun executeStabilizedCapture() {
532 |         // 1. Force Focus Tap
533 |         val metrics = resources.displayMetrics
534 |         val x = metrics.widthPixels / 2f
535 |         val y = metrics.heightPixels / 2f
536 |         val focusPath = Path().apply { moveTo(x, y) }
537 |         dispatchGesture(GestureDescription.Builder().addStroke(GestureDescription.StrokeDescription(focusPath, 0, 40)).build(), null, null)
538 |         
539 |         // 2. Wait for Focus, then Shutter
540 |         handler.postDelayed({
541 |             smartShutterClick()
542 |             captureCount++
543 |             
544 |             if (captureCount < targetCaptureCount) {
545 |                 DebugLogger.log("STABILIZER", "Capture $captureCount success. Resetting for next.")
546 |                 hapticPulse(150) // Confirmation pulse
547 |                 stabilityStartTime = 0
548 |                 handler.postDelayed({ startVibrationLoop() }, 1500)
549 |             } else {
550 |                 stopStabilitySystem()
551 |                 speak("Batch complete", true)
552 |             }
553 |         }, 200)
554 |     }
555 | 
556 |     private fun stopStabilitySystem() {
557 |         isStabilizing = false
558 |         sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
559 |         val v = getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
560 |         v.cancel()
561 |     }
562 | 
563 |     override fun onSensorChanged(event: android.hardware.SensorEvent?) {
564 |         if (event?.sensor?.type == android.hardware.Sensor.TYPE_LINEAR_ACCELERATION) {
565 |             System.arraycopy(event.values, 0, lastLinearAcc, 0, 3)
566 |         } else if (event?.sensor?.type == android.hardware.Sensor.TYPE_GRAVITY) {
567 |             System.arraycopy(event.values, 0, lastGravity, 0, 3)
568 |         }
569 |     }
570 | 
571 |     override fun onAccuracyChanged(sensor: android.hardware.Sensor?, accuracy: Int) {}
572 | 
573 |     private fun smartShutterClick() {
574 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow
575 |         val pkg = root?.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
576 |         val isCamera = pkg.contains("camera") || pkg.contains("lens")
577 | 
578 |         if (!isCamera) {
579 |             DebugLogger.log("SHUTTER", "Blocked: Camera not in foreground ($pkg)")
580 |             speak("Camera is not launched yet", true)
581 |             return
582 |         }
583 | 
584 |         // --- Successive Capture Logic ---
585 |         val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
586 |         val isSuccessive = prefs.getBoolean("successive_enabled", false)
587 |         if (isSuccessive && successiveRemaining == 0) {
588 |             successiveRemaining = prefs.getInt("successive_count", 3) - 1
589 |             triggerSuccessiveLoop()
590 |         }
591 | 
592 |         var foundNode: AccessibilityNodeInfo? = null
593 |         if (root != null) {
594 |             val targets = listOf("shutter", "take picture", "capture", "camera_shutter", "bottom_bar_shutter")
595 |             val queue = LinkedList<AccessibilityNodeInfo>()
596 |             queue.add(root)
597 |             
598 |             while (!queue.isEmpty()) {
599 |                 val node = queue.poll() ?: continue
600 |                 val id = node.viewIdResourceName?.lowercase() ?: ""
601 |                 val desc = node.contentDescription?.toString()?.lowercase() ?: ""
602 |                 
603 |                 if (targets.any { id.contains(it) || desc.contains(it) } && node.isClickable) {
604 |                     foundNode = node
605 |                     break
606 |                 }
607 |                 for (i in 0 until node.childCount) { node.getChild(i)?.let { queue.add(it) } }
608 |             }
609 |         }
610 | 
611 |         if (foundNode != null) {
612 |             DebugLogger.log("AUTO", "Smart Shutter Active")
613 |             if (successiveRemaining <= 0) speak("Capturing image")
614 |             hapticPulse(100)
615 |             foundNode.performAction(android.view.accessibility.AccessibilityNodeInfo.ACTION_CLICK)
616 |         } else {
617 |             DebugLogger.log("AUTO", "Shutter Node Missing")
618 |             if (successiveRemaining <= 0) speak("Shutter not visible, using fallback")
619 |             clickShutterCoordinates()
620 |         }
621 |     }
622 | 
623 |     private fun triggerSuccessiveLoop() {
624 |         if (successiveRemaining > 0) {
625 |             handler.postDelayed({ 
626 |                 DebugLogger.log("AUTO", "Successive Capture: $successiveRemaining left")
627 |                 smartShutterClick()
628 |                 successiveRemaining--
629 |                 triggerSuccessiveLoop()
630 |             }, 1200)
631 |         } else {
632 |             val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
633 |             val total = prefs.getInt("successive_count", 3)
634 |             // The first image was the anchor, so total-1 images are data
635 |             speak("Batch capture complete. ${total - 1} images queued.", true)
636 |             hapticPulse(200)
637 |         }
638 |     }
639 | 
640 |     private fun hapticPulse(ms: Long) {
641 |         try {
642 |             val v = getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
643 |             if (v.hasVibrator()) {
644 |                 // Use low amplitude (50/255) for stealth
645 |                 val stealthMs = if (ms > 100) 60L else ms 
646 |                 v.vibrate(VibrationEffect.createOneShot(stealthMs, 50))
647 |             }
648 |         } catch (e: Exception) { 
649 |             DebugLogger.log("HAPTIC_ERR", "${e.message}")
650 |         }
651 |     }
652 | 
653 |     private fun clickShutterCoordinates() {
654 |         val metrics = resources.displayMetrics
655 |         val x = metrics.widthPixels / 2f
656 |         // Adjusted: 1941/2400 is approx 80%. Using 82% as a safer middle ground for various models.
657 |         val y = metrics.heightPixels * 0.82f
658 | 
659 |         DebugLogger.log("COORD_CLICK", "Targeting fallback coordinates: $x, $y")
660 |         val clickPath = Path().apply { moveTo(x, y) }
661 |         val gesture = GestureDescription.Builder()
662 |             .addStroke(GestureDescription.StrokeDescription(clickPath, 0, 50))
663 |             .build()
664 | 
665 |         dispatchGesture(gesture, object : AccessibilityService.GestureResultCallback() {
666 |             override fun onCompleted(gestureDescription: GestureDescription) {
667 |                 DebugLogger.log("COORD_CLICK", "Point Click SUCCESS at $x, $y")
668 |             }
669 |             override fun onCancelled(gestureDescription: GestureDescription) {
670 |                 DebugLogger.log("COORD_CLICK", "Point Click FAILED at $x, $y")
671 |             }
672 |         }, null)
673 |     }
674 |

676 |         speak("System reset, all data cleared", true)
677 |         val vibrator = getSystemService(Context.VIBRATOR_SERVICE) as Vibrator
678 |         vibrator.vibrate(VibrationEffect.createOneShot(200, VibrationEffect.DEFAULT_AMPLITUDE))
679 |         
680 |         val intent = Intent(this, PlaybackService::class.java)
681 |         intent.action = "RESET"
682 |         startService(intent)
683 |     }

687 |             action = "SPEAK_STATUS"
688 |             putExtra("message", msg)
689 |             putExtra("immediate", immediate)
690 |         }
691 |         startService(intent)
692 |     }

694 |     private fun wakeDevice(pm: PowerManager) {
695 |         DebugLogger.log("WAKE", "Triggering WakeActivity from Key Service")
696 |         val intent = Intent(this, WakeActivity::class.java)
697 |         intent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
698 |         startActivity(intent)
699 |     }

701 |     private fun processHeadsetGesture(sequence: String) {
702 |         if (sequence.isEmpty()) return
703 |         DebugLogger.log("HEADSET_SIG", "Evaluating sequence: '$sequence' | NavMode: $isEarphoneNavMode")
704 |         val intent = Intent(this, PlaybackService::class.java)
705 |         val pm = getSystemService(Context.POWER_SERVICE) as PowerManager
706 |         
707 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow
708 |         val pkg = root?.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
709 |         val isCam = pkg.contains("camera") || pkg.contains("lens")
710 | 
711 |         // --- GLOBAL PRIORITY COMMANDS ---
712 | 
713 |         // 1. Wake (Screen Off + Single Click)
714 |         if (!pm.isInteractive && sequence == "S") {
715 |             wakeDevice(pm)
716 |             return
717 |         }
718 | 
719 |         // 2. Global Lock (Triple Click ALWAYS locks screen)
720 |         if (sequence == "SSS") {
721 |             DebugLogger.log("HEADSET", "Global Lock Triggered")
722 |             if (android.os.Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES.P) {
723 |                 performGlobalAction(AccessibilityService.GLOBAL_ACTION_LOCK_SCREEN)
724 |             } else {
725 |                 speak("Lock feature requires Android 9", true)
726 |             }
727 |             return
728 |         }
729 | 
730 |         // 3. Global Reset (Long-Short ALWAYS clears session)
731 |         if (sequence == "LS") {
732 |             DebugLogger.log("HEADSET", "Global Session Reset Triggered")
733 |             val resetIntent = Intent(this, PlaybackService::class.java).apply { action = "RESET" }
734 |             startService(resetIntent)
735 |             return
736 |         }
737 | 
738 |         // 4. Camera Shutter Priority
739 |         if (isCam && sequence == "S") {
740 |             DebugLogger.log("HEADSET", "Camera detected: Overriding 'S' to Shutter")
741 |             smartShutterClick()
742 |             return
743 |         }
744 | 
745 |         // --- MODE SPECIFIC COMMANDS ---
746 | 
747 |         if (isEarphoneNavMode) {
748 |             when (sequence) {
749 |                 "S" -> intent.action = "PAUSE"
750 |                 "SS" -> intent.action = "NEXT"
751 |                 "L" -> intent.action = "NEXT_CATEGORY"
752 |                 "SL" -> {
753 |                     DebugLogger.log("HEADSET", "Nav Mode: Triggering SL Camera Toggle")
754 |                     toggleCamera()
755 |                     return
756 |                 }
757 |                 else -> {
758 |                    if (sequence.contains("L")) intent.action = "NEXT_CATEGORY"
759 |                    else intent.action = "NEXT"
760 |                 }
761 |             }
762 |             if (intent.action != null) startService(intent)
763 |         } else {
764 |             when (sequence) {
765 |                 "S" -> smartShutterClick()
766 |                 "SS" -> toggleCamera()
767 |                 "L" -> {
768 |                     speak("Earphone trigger: Initiating batch upload", true)
769 |                     val intentBatch = Intent(this@KeyInterceptService, ImageMonitorService::class.java).apply { 
770 |                         action = "COMMAND_FLUSH_BATCH" 
771 |                     }
772 |                     startService(intentBatch)
773 |                 }
774 |                 else -> speak("$sequence detected", true)
775 |             }
776 |         }
777 |         
778 |         DebugLogger.log("HEADSET_NAV", "Mode: $isEarphoneNavMode, Seq: $sequence")
779 |     }
780 | 
781 |     private fun logUiHierarchy() {
782 |         val root = rootInActiveWindow ?: return
783 |         DebugLogger.log("UI_DUMP", "--- Camera UI Start ---")
784 |         recursiveLog(root, 0)
785 |         DebugLogger.log("UI_DUMP", "--- Camera UI End ---")
786 |     }
787 | 
788 |     private fun recursiveLog(node: AccessibilityNodeInfo, depth: Int) {
789 |         if (depth > 15) return
790 |         val sb = StringBuilder()
791 |         repeat(depth) { sb.append(".") }
792 |         
793 |         val text = node.text?.toString() ?: ""
794 |         val desc = node.contentDescription?.toString() ?: ""
795 |         val id = node.viewIdResourceName ?: ""
796 |         val bounds = Rect()
797 |         node.getBoundsInScreen(bounds)
798 | 
799 |         sb.append("[").append(node.className?.toString()?.split(".")?.last() ?: "Unknown").append("] ")
800 |         if (text.isNotEmpty()) sb.append("T:$text ")
801 |         if (desc.isNotEmpty()) sb.append("D:$desc ")
802 |         if (id.isNotEmpty()) sb.append("ID:$id ")
803 |         sb.append("B:(${bounds.left},${bounds.top})")
804 |         
805 |         if (node.isClickable || text.contains("0") || desc.contains("0")) {
806 |              DebugLogger.log("UI_TRACE", sb.toString())
807 |         }
808 |         
809 |         for (i in 0 until node.childCount) {
810 |             node.getChild(i)?.let { recursiveLog(it, depth + 1) }
811 |         }
812 |     }
813 | 
814 |     override fun onAccessibilityEvent(event: AccessibilityEvent?) {
815 |         val prefs = getSharedPreferences("monitor_prefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
816 |         val shouldBlock = prefs.getBoolean("touch_blocker", false)
817 | 
818 |         // 1. Enforce Global Blocker Policy
819 |         if (shouldBlock && blockerOverlay == null) {
820 |             showTouchBlocker()
821 |         }
822 | 
823 |         val type = event?.eventType
824 |         val pkg = event?.packageName?.toString() ?: ""
825 |         val isCam = pkg.contains("camera") || pkg.contains("lens")
826 | 
827 |         if (isCam) {
828 |             if (!prefs.getBoolean("is_active", false)) return
829 | 
830 |             if (type == AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGED ||
